children.heading: Applicability
children.label: a Applicability
children.text: The provisions of this section shall apply where coal owned by the United States under land the surface rights to which are owned by a surface owner as defined in this section is to be mined by methods other than underground mining techniques.

children.heading: Lease of coal deposits governed bysection 201 of this title
children.label: b Lease of coal deposits governed bysection 201 of this title
children.text: Any coal deposits subject to this section shall be offered for lease pursuant to .section 201(a) of this title

children.heading: Consent to lease by surface owner
children.label: c Consent to lease by surface owner
children.text: The Secretary shall not enter into any lease of Federal coal deposits until the surface owner has given written consent to enter and commence surface mining operations and the Secretary has obtained evidence of such consent. Valid written consent given by any surface owner prior to , shall be deemed sufficient for the purposes of complying with this section.August 3, 1977

children.heading: Preferences
children.label: d Preferences
children.text: In order to minimize disturbance to surface owners from surface coal mining of Federal coal deposits and to assist in the preparation of comprehensive land-use plans required by , the Secretary shall consult with any surface owner whose land is proposed to be included in a leasing tract and shall ask the surface owner to state his preference for or against the offering of the deposit under his land for lease. The Secretary shall, in his discretion but to the maximum extent practicable, refrain from leasing coal deposits for development by methods other than underground mining techniques in those areas where a significant number of surface owners have stated a preference against the offering of the deposits for lease.section 201(a) of this title
hold legal or equitable title to the land surface;

have their principal place of residence on the land; or personally conduct farming or ranching operations upon a farm or ranch unit to be affected by surface coal mining operations; or receive directly a significant portion of their income, if any, from such farming or ranching operations; and
have met the conditions of paragraphs (1) and (2) for a period of at least three years prior to the granting of the consent.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: f Exception
children.text: This section shall not apply to Indian lands.

children.heading: Effect on property rights of United States or any other landowner
children.label: g Effect on property rights of United States or any other landowner
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as increasing or diminishing any property rights by the United States or by any other landowner.
